question: Based on these maps, what can be concluded about which type of unemployment has higher average rates across states?

options.A: The maps show that youth unemployment clearly has higher average rates than overall unemployment
options.B: The maps show that overall unemployment clearly has higher average rates than youth unemployment
options.C: Both youth and overall unemployment have similar average rates across states
options.D: The maps do not provide enough information to determine which type has higher average rates

explanation: Both maps reuse the same threshold scale, but their underlying distributions differ. This can make one map appear more intense simply because more states fall into the top bin. In the underlying data, Youth Unemployment has the higher average value, so that is the only reliable conclusion.
